comment: F27 P9 T1j — acceptance floors lifted after global bipartite matching shipped. Measured 2026-05-18 with score_eval.py defaults (--span-expansion 15 --matcher bipartite): macro recall 0.639 (was T1i 0.583), macro precision_highest_stage 0.716 (was 0.661), F1_highest 0.677, per-PR recall min 0.500, per-PR precision_highest min 0.500. T1j replaces T1i's iterate-over-expected greedy matcher with globally optimal max-weight bipartite matching (scipy.optimize.linear_sum_assignment) under a cardinality-first integer-encoded objective: matches > original_overlap > expanded_overlap > deterministic tiebreaker on declared order. Cardinality dominance is computed against matrix-wide totals (not per-cell) so the matcher provably never trades two weak matches for one strong match. This unlocks safe span_expansion to N=15 (was 5 under greedy where N>=7 introduced PR-977882 sk-3 pair theft between s09 and s04). PR-976609 gained one match (recall 0.500 -> 0.667 via s05 unlocked by N=15 expansion); PR-975848 and PR-977882 unchanged but with provably-optimal assignment. Floors sit at ~78-82% of measured per rubber-duck recommendation (closer to documented 70-80% than aggressive 86% would have been). Spec's nominal 80% recall / 90% precision is still the destination; remaining headroom lives in curator augmentation of the 3-PR corpus toward 8-10 PRs (T1k) plus potential T2 work on Architect prompt for the s04/s09 ambiguity. `enforcement: report_only` until the corpus crosses 0.70 macro recall.
